label: Fixtures
description: Fixture list with FilterBar, live pulse indicator, scores, and row selection for match detail.

0.5.note: FOOTBALL_LEAGUES re-exported from sports-data-context; widget imports from context, not lib/mocks.

0.6.note: No async loading path (synchronous filtered fixtures).

0.7.note: Empty state: EmptyState renders when totalCount===0.

0.8.note: No error path in context (mock-only architecture).

1.1.note: Data via useSportsData().filteredFixtures — no inline fetch.

1.2.note: Context calls useGlobalScope() and uses organizationIds.

1.3.note: Org filter gates bets (hasSportsDesk); fixtures are public events.

1.4.note: No client-level scope for fixture data.

1.6.note: Empty scope arrays → applyFilters returns all fixtures.

1.7.note: FOOTBALL_LEAGUES moved to context re-export; no reference data in widget.

l1.notes: All checks pass. Fixture data is global (venue-level), not org-scoped — correct domain behavior.

l2.notes: Covered globally by commits 32ba8ab and f9f29ea. Re-audit per-widget only if the wiring test fails or entitlements are added.

3.1.note: Mode relevance: live ✓ (real-time fixture list), paper ✓ (same view, no trade submission), batch ✓ (only completed fixtures shown via isBatch filter).

3.2.note: Context reads useExecutionMode() → isBatch; filteredFixtures filters completed-only in batch mode.

3.5.note: Paper mode: fixture list displays normally; bet placement hidden at trade panel level (not this widget). Expected: same fixture list, no trade UI.

3.6.note: No WebSocket or interval in this widget; WebSocket is in sports-data-context and hooks/use-sports-live-updates (not widget-level — context-level responsibility).

l3.notes: Agent checks 3.1/3.2/3.5/3.6 pass. Human checks 3.3/3.4 pending: verify batch mode shows completed-only in browser + batch indicator visible.

4.1.note: No dead buttons — all onClick handlers perform meaningful state updates (setSelectedFixtureId, setFilters, toggleLeague, setShowFilters).

4.2.note: No href='#' or dead links — widget uses buttons only, no anchor links.

4.5.note: No links to gated pages — widget is self-contained.

4.6.note: No entitlement checks in render — handled at widget-wrapper level.

l4.notes: Agent checks 4.1/4.2/4.5/4.6 pass. Human checks 4.3/4.4/4.7/4.8 pending: verify filter fields complete, fixture selection works, filter bar toggles, scroll within bounds.

5.1.note: Target strategies: sports-arb (fixture selection feeds arb scanner), sports-betting (fixture selection feeds bet placement via fixture-detail). Not a strategy execution widget itself — it is a fixture browser/selector.

l5.notes: Agent check 5.1 complete. Human checks 5.2-5.6 pending: verify fixture selection drives fixture-detail correctly, all league filters work, search narrows correctly.

l6.notes: L6 deferred — tests not yet written for sports domain.

7.1.note: No hardcoded hex/rgb/hsl colors found in widget file.

7.2.note: No mock imports in widget — FOOTBALL_LEAGUES sourced from context re-export.

7.3.note: 233 lines — within 500 limit. No single function exceeds 200 lines.

7.4.note: Component PascalCase (SportsFixturesWidget), hooks useCamelCase, file kebab-case.

7.5.note: Out of scope — English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21; 7.5 reassigned to UTC datetime rendering

7.6.note: No dangerouslySetInnerHTML, eval, new Function, or innerHTML= found.

7.7.note: All useMemo/useCallback deps are stable refs. No inline object/array literals in effect/memo deps.

8.1.note: No text-[] or fontSize arbitrary values found.

8.2.note: No spacing arbitrary values (p-[], m-[], gap-[]) found.

8.3.note: Uses FilterBar, EmptyState, LeagueBadge shared primitives; native <button> elements used — correct.

8.4.note: Agent: no without alt, no interactive divs without role — all interactive elements are

8.5.note: Human-only: resize test at minW=4/minH=6 pending.

l8.notes: 8.1-8.3 pass. 8.4 agent portion pass. 8.4-8.5 human walkthrough pending.

keepSeparate.rationale: Fixtures is a list/browser widget; fixture-detail is a detail panel. Keeping separate follows master/detail pattern used across the platform.

relationships.notes: Fixtures widget sets selectedFixtureId via SportsDataContext, which sports-fixture-detail consumes. Also drives arb scanner tab navigation via handleViewArb.

dataContract.notes: Live updates delivered via WebSocket hook; base fixture list loaded from mock/REST. In production: initial REST load + WS overlay for score/status updates.

gaps: Only EPL/UCL/LaLiga/BL/L1 leagues covered in mock data; international competitions not represented
No pagination for large fixture sets
coverage.notes: Widget renders all fixture leagues present in mock data. League filter tested manually.

findings.description: sports-fixtures-widget.tsx — FOOTBALL_LEAGUES was imported from lib/mocks/fixtures/sports-fixtures
findings.fix: FOOTBALL_LEAGUES re-exported from sports-data-context; widget imports from context
Retired 7.5 i18n check: English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21
